title: mentha arvensis var. domita
intro: Mentha arvensis var. domita, also known as Japanese peppermint, is a variety of the wild mint plant, native to Japan and other parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe digestive issues and relieve headaches. The plant's health benefits include reducing nausea and vomiting, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. Mentha arvensis var. domita can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
